title: Use native WSL systemd support
body:
WSL can run systemd natively ([Systemd support is now available in WSL!](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/systemd-support-is-now-available-in-wsl/)). 

One can opt-in to enable systemd by adding 
```
[boot]
systemd=true
```
to `/etc/wsl.conf`.

Currently, systemd is enabled by using a nested process namespace. This was a typical workaround, that should no longer be necessary. 

Using the native systemd support should make podman-wsl-fedora behave more similar to other WSL distributions and Fedora Linux systems. (E.g., issues like https://github.com/containers/podman-wsl-fedora/issues/9 would be avoided.)

comments.body: The bootstrap script at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/wsl/machine.go#L73 creates the nested process namespace for systemd. This file contains a lot more special handling of systemd for WSL that should no longer be necessary once the native systemd support is used instead.

Thanks for reaching out, @Karsten7 !

@n1hility, what do you think?
